hero.headline: JAIN Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) Online MBA with a Human Resource Management specialisation. NIRF #62 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Talent Acquisition, Learning and Development, Industrial Relations and Labour Laws, Employee Management and Negotiation Skills, International HRM, and Performance Management in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: JAIN Online MBA HRM: NIRF #62, NAAC A++, fees Rs 1.75L-1.96L (3 payment options), 4 scholarship categories. HR depth with Talent Acquisition, L&D, Industrial Relations, Employee Negotiation Skills, International HRM, Performance Management, and OD. Rare dedicated Employee Negotiation Skills subject. Strong for HRBP and HR manager careers.

about.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in Human Resource Management is offered by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University), Bengaluru. JAIN holds NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #62 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #73 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The HRM specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Talent Acquisition and Management, Learning and Development, Industrial Relations and Labour Laws, Employee Management and Negotiation Skills, International HRM, Performance Management Systems, and Organisation Development and Change Management.

Employee Management and Negotiation Skills as a dedicated HR subject is uncommon among peer online MBA programmes. This subject directly targets the most frequently tested HR business partner competency: managing difficult employee conversations, performance improvement processes, and grievance resolution. Combined with Python for Data Science in Sem 2 core, JAIN HR students graduate with both people management depth and basic analytics capabilities.

sem3Subjects.name: Business Research Methods
sem3Subjects.description: Design HR research studies, conduct workforce surveys, and apply statistical analysis to people management decisions.

sem3Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply process design and quality frameworks to HR operations, shared service centre design, and HR function management.

sem3Subjects.name: Talent Acquisition and Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build talent acquisition strategy, manage employer branding, design structured interview processes, and develop talent pipelines.

sem3Subjects.name: Learning and Development
sem3Subjects.description: Design L&D frameworks, build capability programmes, manage LMS platforms, and evaluate training ROI for L&D management roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Industrial Relations and Labour Laws
sem3Subjects.description: Apply Industrial Disputes Act, Contract Labour Act, POSH compliance, and collective bargaining for HR compliance roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Employee Management and Negotiation Skills
sem3Subjects.description: Manage employee grievances, conduct performance improvement discussions, and negotiate employment terms and dispute resolution.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Align HR strategy with business objectives, develop workforce planning models, and design HR-led transformation programmes.

sem4Subjects.name: International HRM
sem4Subjects.description: Design expatriate management policies, global mobility frameworks, and cross-cultural talent management for multinational HR roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Performance Management Systems
sem4Subjects.description: Build KPI frameworks, manage appraisal calibration cycles, design continuous feedback systems, and implement bell curve management.

sem4Subjects.name: Organization Development and Change Management
sem4Subjects.description: Lead OD interventions, manage large-scale culture transformation, and apply change management frameworks to HR-led programmes.

sem4Subjects.name: Employee Compensation and Benefits
sem4Subjects.description: Design CTC structures, manage pay bands, implement variable pay, and conduct compensation benchmarking for compensation roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Master Thesis / Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied HR research project on talent management, OD intervention, or compensation benchmarking strategy.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all JAIN MBA specialisations. See the main JAIN Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Human Resource Management MBA graduates from JAIN Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** IT services and product companies, BFSI, FMCG, Manufacturing, Consulting and staffing, EdTech

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Executive, L&D Coordinator, HR Generalist, Compensation Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Business Partner, Talent Acquisition Manager, L&D Manager, Compensation Manager, HR Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** HR Director, CHRO, Head of Talent Management, VP HR, Head of People Operations

**Top hiring organisations:** Infosys, Wipro, TCS, HCL Technologies, Accenture India, IBM India, Amazon India HR, Flipkart People, HDFC Bank HR, Deloitte HR Consulting, KPMG People Advisory, ManpowerGroup, Randstad India

skills.body:
The JAIN HRM specialisation builds skills that HR employers test at HRBP and HR manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Industrial Relations and Labour Laws: Industrial Disputes Act compliance, POSH implementation, Contract Labour Act management, and collective bargaining for HR compliance and IR roles
- Performance Management Systems: KPI framework design, appraisal calibration, continuous feedback tool implementation, and performance improvement plan management for HRBP roles
- Employee Compensation and Benefits: CTC structuring, pay band design, variable pay programme management, and compensation benchmarking for compensation and benefits roles
- Talent Acquisition and Management: employer branding strategy, structured interview design, talent pipeline development, and ATS management for talent acquisition roles

**Applied skills:**
- Employee Management and Negotiation Skills: grievance management, PIP facilitation, employment dispute resolution, and negotiation in difficult HR conversations - a dedicated subject uncommon in peer programmes
- Organisation Development and Change Management: OD intervention design, culture transformation, and HR-led change management

**Soft skills:** Conducting sensitive performance and compensation conversations, presenting workforce analytics to CHRO and board, managing union relations, and leading employer brand campaigns.

comparisons.body:
Comparing JAIN Online MBA HRM against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA HRM:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000 with strong all-India HR brand recognition. JAIN HRM at Rs 1,75,000-1,96,000 adds a dedicated Employee Management and Negotiation Skills subject - a standalone competency development subject not found in Amity's HRM curriculum. For South India IT services HR careers, JAIN carries stronger Bengaluru ecosystem access.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA HRM:** NMIMS carries strong Mumbai brand recognition in BFSI and corporate HR at Rs 1,60,000-1,95,000. For Mumbai corporate HR careers, NMIMS carries stronger recognition. JAIN HRM includes Learning and Development as a dedicated Sem 3 subject and a standalone Employee Negotiation Skills subject. For Bengaluru IT and consulting HR roles, JAIN is competitive.

**vs LPU Online MBA HRM:** LPU is NIRF #31 at Rs 1,61,600 with zero-cost EMI and dedicated HR Analytics as a Sem 4 subject. JAIN HRM includes Employee Negotiation Skills and Learning and Development as distinct subjects. JAIN carries stronger South India brand recognition; LPU is stronger in North India. Both offer comparable subject depth at similar fee levels.

fees.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in Human Resource Management is priced between Rs 1,75,000 and Rs 1,96,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year program). JAIN offers different fee tiers based on specialisation - contact our counsellor for exact fee for this specific specialisation.

**Payment options (3 ways)**:

- **Semester-wise**: Rs 43,750 per semester (Tier 1) or Rs 49,000 per semester (Tier 2)
- **Yearly**: Rs 87,500 per year (Tier 1) or Rs 98,000 per year (Tier 2)
- **One-time upfront**: Rs 1,75,000 (Tier 1) or Rs 1,96,000 (Tier 2)

**Additional charges (Indian students)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 2,500 one-time
- Examination fee: Rs 3,000 per year (Rs 6,000 total for 2 years)

**Total effective cost (Indian student)**:
- Tier 1 specs: Rs 1,83,500
- Tier 2 specs: Rs 2,04,500

**International student fees**:
- SAARC countries: additional Rs 11,250 registration + Rs 3,750 per year exam fee
- Other international students: USD 150 registration + USD 50 per year exam fee

**EMI options**: Available through JAIN's direct NBFC lending partner tie-ups (same pattern as LPU and MUJ - not through external marketing partners). Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options, approval terms, and whether zero-cost or interest-bearing.

**Scholarships available** (4 categories - specific discount % and eligibility varies by admission cycle):
- **Merit-based**: Academic excellence scholarship for qualifying marks
- **Armed Forces**: Support for serving/veteran military personnel and families
- **Gender-focused**: Empowerment scholarship for diverse applicants
- **Freshers**: Transition support for recent undergraduates

Specific discount percentages are confirmed at admission based on eligibility. Contact our counsellor for current cycle scholarship details.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with JAIN each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) and carries the JAIN name with the MBA designation. The Human Resource Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from JAIN Online MBA HRM alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Ananya Krishnan
items.liked: Employee Management and Negotiation Skills was the standout subject - no other online MBA I evaluated had this as a dedicated module. Industrial Relations and Labour Laws was directly applicable to my IT company HR role.
items.disliked: International HRM could include more India-headquartered MNC case studies.
items.body: I had 4 years in HR operations at a Bengaluru IT company and the JAIN HRM MBA helped me move to HRBP. Python in Sem 2 core helped me build basic workforce dashboards that impressed the HR leadership team.

items.name: Pradeep Rao
items.liked: Talent Acquisition and L&D subjects were well-structured. Performance Management Systems was directly applicable.
items.disliked: The fee tier was not immediately clear during admission. Needed counsellor clarification.
items.body: Good programme for South India IT services HR careers. Hyderabad IT companies recognised the JAIN credential. For pan-India HR careers, Amity or NMIMS would add national brand recognition. For South India, JAIN is cost-effective.

items.name: Sneha Thomas
items.liked: OD and Change Management subject gave strong frameworks for culture transformation projects.
items.disliked: LMS platform had occasional stability issues. Live session recordings were sometimes delayed beyond 24 hours.
items.body: Decent credential for the fee. JAIN brand is strong in Bengaluru and Hyderabad but less so in Kerala. For South India HR careers, self-sourcing was necessary outside major metros.

faqs.question: How are JAIN fees different for different specializations?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers two pricing tiers across its MBA specialisations. Tier 1 is Rs 1,75,000 total (plus Rs 2,500 registration and Rs 6,000 examination fees). Tier 2 is Rs 1,96,000 total (same additional charges). The tier applicable to the HRM specialisation should be confirmed with our counsellor as tier assignment can change across admission cycles.

faqs.question: What scholarships does JAIN offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers 4 scholarship categories: Merit-based (academic excellence), Armed Forces (military personnel and families), Gender-focused (diversity empowerment), and Freshers (recent graduates). Specific discount percentages and eligibility conditions are confirmed at admission and vary by intake cycle. Contact our counsellor for current cycle details.

faqs.question: Does JAIN offer dual specialization in Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. For HR-focused students, JAIN offers Marketing and HRM (dual), HRM and Finance (dual), and HR and Business Analytics (dual) specialisations. These dual specs cover subjects from both domains in Sem 3-4, giving more career versatility than single-spec HRM programmes at peer universities.

faqs.question: Is Employee Negotiation Skills a unique subject in JAIN HRM?
faqs.answer: Yes. Employee Management and Negotiation Skills is a dedicated Sem 3 subject covering grievance management, performance improvement plan facilitation, employment dispute resolution, and negotiation in difficult HR conversations. This is uncommon as a standalone subject in peer online MBA HRM programmes and directly tests the most frequently required HRBP competency.

faqs.question: Is JAIN HRM MBA suitable for IT company HR professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. Talent Acquisition, Performance Management Systems, and L&D subjects are directly applicable to IT services HR roles. JAIN's Bengaluru base gives strong alumni connect with Bengaluru and Hyderabad IT companies. Python for Data Science in Sem 2 core equips HR professionals with basic workforce analytics skills increasingly required in IT sector HR roles.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A++ HRM MBA with dedicated Employee Negotiation Skills and Learning and Development subjects.
You are targeting HR business partner or HR manager roles in South India IT services or FMCG companies.
You want 3 flexible payment options and 4 scholarship categories at fees of Rs 1.75L-1.96L.
consider: If pan-India HR brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA HRM at Rs 2.25L or NMIMS HRM offer stronger national recognition.
If dedicated HR Analytics as a subject is required, LPU Online MBA HRM includes HR Analytics as a Sem 4 subject at Rs 1,61,600 with zero-cost EMI.
If dual HR specialisation with finance or analytics is preferred, JAIN's HRM and Finance or HR and Business Analytics dual specs cover both domains.
